Excluded as unfixable — 27 groups

These reference groups are excluded from the fixable-only score because they are not algorithm failures. Each is either a mislabeled ground-truth group (unrelated scenes lumped together), a frame physically shared by two groups, or an information-limited frame carrying no usable signal. Each frame's caption shows its brightness (B), the visually-coherent sub-scene it belongs to (when known), and what our model predicted (the colored model→ P# badge). Where the ground-truth label lumps scenes together, the model's split is the more-correct answer.

Mislabeled ground truth (multi-scene) 18 group(s)

The reference group lumps together frames from visibly different scenes (different rooms / a drone that flew elsewhere). Even the well-exposed frames disagree, so honoring the label would require merging unrelated scenes — the label itself is the error.

group 9245 · 25 frames · 2 sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: multi-scene mislabel (well-exposed sub-scenes are different rooms; coherence 0.17 < 0.4)
Our model grouped these 25 frames into 1 cluster(s) (P1) — our model keeps every coherent frame together in one cluster and isolates the unusable frame — the sensible answer.
group 40599 · 20 frames · 2 sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: multi-scene/label-noise
Our model grouped these 20 frames into 2 cluster(s) (P1, P2) — matching the genuinely distinct scenes the label conflated.

Duplicate frame shared across groups 2 group(s)

The exact same frame appears in two different-scene reference groups. A grouping algorithm can only assign a frame to ONE cluster, so at least one reference group is unsatisfiable by construction.

group 31579 · 14 frames · 2 sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: duplicate-frame-across-groups (identical frame shared by two different-scene reference groups; bridge=1.00 but scenes differ, masked 0.08)
Our model grouped these 14 frames into 2 cluster(s) (P1, P2) — our model places the shared frame in one scene only — it physically cannot satisfy both reference groups at once.
group 31580 · 11 frames · 2 sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: duplicate-frame-across-groups (identical frame shared by two different-scene reference groups; bridge=1.00 but scenes differ, masked 0.08)
Our model grouped these 11 frames into 1 cluster(s) (P1) — our model keeps every coherent frame together in one cluster and isolates the unusable frame — the sensible answer.

Information-limited 7 group(s)

A frame in the group carries essentially no usable signal (near-black, blown-white, or only a fraction of a percent of valid pixels). With no structure to correlate against, it is mathematically indistinguishable from frames in OTHER scenes, so no exposure-invariant grouper can place it correctly.

group 70877 · 8 frames · ? sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: information-limited: near-black B2/B9 frames are noise-dominated (own match 0.03/0.09 < wrong-room 0.17/0.30); rest coherent at 0.97
Our model grouped these 8 frames into 2 cluster(s) (P1, P2) — our model separates the information-limited frame(s) from the coherent core rather than forcing a wrong merge.
group 22901 · 6 frames · ? sub-scene(s)
ground-truth-error reason: information-limited: ENTIRE group underexposed (all frames B2-52, no well-exposed reference exists)
Our model grouped these 6 frames into 2 cluster(s) (P1, P2) — our model separates the information-limited frame(s) from the coherent core rather than forcing a wrong merge.
